Title: High-frequency, precise modification of the tomato genome

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation; excessive accumulation associated with growth inhibition
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promoter insertion upstream of endogenous ANT1 causes overexpression and ectopic anthocyanin accumulation, producing purple tissues
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted promoter insertion by homologous recombination using geminivirus replicons with TALENs or CRISPR/Cas9; PCR genotyping of left/right junctions; sequencing; Southern blot; segregation analysis in T1; visual pigment phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates anthocyanin biosynthetic pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation via overexpression after 35S promoter knock-in
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: ANT1 is the focal decisive regulator used as a visible metabolic switch; overexpression was sufficient to induce strong anthocyanin accumulation in multiple tomato tissues
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ectopic overexpression in transformed purple callus, flowers, fruit, foliage, and embryos/seedlings after 35S promoter insertion
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Kanamycin selection during callus induction enabled recovery of targeted events; no purple spots without selection
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: 35S promoter insertion upstream of ANT1 coding sequence
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excessive anthocyanin accumulation caused reduced growth, especially in homozygous T1 plants; possible underrepresentation of homozygotes and possible effects on seed viability/germination
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Amplicon deep sequencing of ANT1 target locus in tomato protoplasts using Illumina MiSeq; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ANT1 was the only core metabolism-related gene experimentally targeted in the study. 2. Insertion of a constitutive 35S promoter upstream of endogenous ANT1 by gene targeting caused ANT1 overexpression and strong anthocyanin accumulation in tomato tissues. 3. The targeted ANT1 modification was often precise, heritable, and segregated largely as a Mendelian allele. 4. Homozygous ANT1 overexpression lines showed stronger pigmentation and growth inhibition.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful visible marker and proof-of-concept regulatory target for precise metabolic engineering of flavonoid pigmentation in tomato; should be deployed cautiously because strong constitutive activation can impair growth, especially in homozygotes
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper is primarily a genome editing methodology study rather than a metabolism discovery study; ANT1 is the sole nutrient/metabolism-relevant core gene in the Results. TALENs, Cas9, Rep/RepA, NPTII and viral factors are tools/background components, not core metabolism genes.
